prompts: Earth's rotation refers to the spinning of the planet around its own axis, an imaginary line passing through the North and South Poles, which takes approximately 24 hours and results in day and night cycles. Earth's revolution is its orbit around the Sun, which takes about 365.25 days to complete, defining one year and causing the seasonal changes due to the axial tilt of approximately 23.5 degrees. This axial tilt means that different parts of Earth receive varying amounts of sunlight throughout the year, leading to variations in temperature and the succession of seasons.
